test_output_summary: 980 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed in 3.62s. New file tests/test_assemble_tool_list.py contributes 29 tests covering all five acceptance criteria; full Phase 1 + Phase 2 suite continues to pass.
implementation_notes: Populated src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py to expose the 15-symbol public surface listed in API-internal.md §1.1 (4 Pydantic types + 4 general tools + 3 capability catalogue tools + 2 dispatch tools + assemble_tool_list + load_stub_registry). The new assemble_tool_list(config, capability_registry) factory is the single wiring point: it (a) calls jarvis.tools.general.configure(config) so search_web can resolve the active JarvisConfig, (b) snapshot-copies the registry into capabilities._capability_registry and dispatch._capability_registry as two independent list(...) copies (ASSUM-006 isolation), and (c) returns the 9 tools in stable alphabetical order. To prevent regressions to existing invariants I removed jarvis.tools from RESERVED_PACKAGES in tests/test_reserved_packages.py and tests/test_import_graph.py (the package is no longer reserved-empty after this task) and replaced the empty-namespace contract with a stricter AC-005 check inside the new test module: production code under src/jarvis/ outside src/jarvis/tools/ may import jarvis.tools but never the submodules general/capabilities/dispatch.
concerns: API-internal.md §1.1 lists 15 public symbols, but the AC-001 wording says '11 symbols plus assemble_tool_list and load_stub_registry' (=13). I followed the canonical API listing (15) rather than the round count in the AC text, since the AC explicitly defers to API-internal.md §1.1 for the exact set.

test_import_graph.py still hard-codes 'jarvis.tools' in TestStaticImportGraph.test_specific_reserved_not_imported_by_any_domain_module's parametrize list. No domain module imports jarvis.tools today, so the test still passes — but downstream wiring tasks (e.g. lifecycle.startup importing assemble_tool_list) will need to remove that entry.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py` re-exports exactly the public surface listed in API-internal.md §1.1 (11 symbols plus `assemble_tool_list` and `load_stub_registry`).
completion_promises.evidence: __init__.py defines __all__ with the 15 symbols enumerated in API-internal.md §1.1 (CalendarEvent, CapabilityDescriptor, DispatchError, WebResult, calculate, get_calendar_events, read_file, search_web, capabilities_refresh, capabilities_subscribe_updates, list_available_capabilities, dispatch_by_capability, queue_build, assemble_tool_list, load_stub_registry). TestAC001PublicSurface.test_all_attribute_matches_expected_surface and the parametrized test_each_symbol_is_importable lock the set against drift.

assemble_tool_list is defined in __init__.py with the documented signature and a docstring describing its three side effects. TestAC002Signature.test_returns_list_of_base_tools verifies the return type at runtime (length 9, every entry isinstance(BaseTool)) and test_accepts_empty_registry exercises the empty-registry branch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `assemble_tool_list` returns the 9 tools in stable alphabetical order: `calculate, capabilities_refresh, capabilities_subscribe_updates, dispatch_by_capability, get_calendar_events, list_available_capabilities, queue_build, read_file, search_web`.
completion_promises.evidence: The function returns a literal list in the documented order. TestAC003AlphabeticalOrder.test_tool_names_match_expected_order asserts the names are exactly the documented sequence; test_order_is_idempotent_across_calls confirms repeated invocations yield identical ordering.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `assemble_tool_list` is the **only** place that binds capability_registry into the capability + dispatch tools via closure (snapshot isolation).
completion_promises.evidence: assemble_tool_list rebinds capabilities._capability_registry and dispatch._capability_registry to fresh list(capability_registry) copies — no other code in src/jarvis/ touches those attributes. TestAC004SnapshotIsolation covers: (a) both modules receive the snapshot, (b) the snapshots are independent list objects, (c) mutating the operator's outer list after assemble does not leak into either module, (d) general._config is set to the passed config, (e) end-to-end the catalogue tool surfaces the assembled descriptor.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] No other module imports `jarvis.tools.general`, `jarvis.tools.capabilities`, `jarvis.tools.dispatch` directly — only `jarvis.tools`.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC005NoSubmoduleImports walks every .py file under src/jarvis/ outside src/jarvis/tools/ and asserts no AST import targets jarvis.tools.general, jarvis.tools.capabilities, or jarvis.tools.dispatch. Intra-package sibling imports (dispatch.py importing capabilities.py, etc.) are explicitly skipped — they are required for the implementation to compose. Updated tests/test_reserved_packages.py and tests/test_import_graph.py to remove jarvis.tools from the reserved-empty list since the package is now populated; the boundary discipline is enforced by the new submodule-import test instead.
